name: developer-docs
genre: The prototypical developer documentation site: a calm, high-legibility light reading surface with a persistent left sidebar, a roughly 65ch prose column, one blue accent for links and active state, and code surfaces clearly differentiated from prose. Structure is maximally conventional; novelty is spent only on the reading experience.

provenance.sample: category convention: documentation sites (persistent left sidebar, prose column, right on-this-page rail); no single-source sample file for this genre
provenance.evidence_note: Docs are read, not skimmed, so the whole design serves legibility: high text contrast, a measured reading column, and code that never fights the prose. Low visual complexity plus high prototypicality wins the 50ms first impression (style.md); a docs page should look exactly like a docs page, so all novelty budget goes into typography and one calm accent, not layout invention. Blue is the lowest-risk trust accent (color.md); admonition hues are held distinct from it so a warning never reads as a link. Committed to a light ground for long-form reading comfort; treat light/dark as an audience parameter, not a universal.

ground-0.role: page ground; near-white with the faintest cool cast, never pure #FFFFFF, so raised panels can still read as raised

ground-1.role: raised panel ground: the left sidebar, the on-this-page rail, table header rows

ground-2.role: fenced code-block surface; a clearly cooler and darker step so code reads as a distinct surface from prose

code-inline.role: inline code chip background; sits inside body text and must still hold 4.5:1 for the code glyphs on top of it

accent-soft.role: the active sidebar item fill; a pale accent tint, meaningful only in combination with the accent-colored label and the left accent bar

ink.role: primary body text and headings; a warm-neutral near-black, never pure #000, holds well above 4.5:1 on every ground

code-text.role: monospace glyphs inside inline chips and code blocks; a shade lighter than ink so code reads as its own register while still exceeding 4.5:1

muted.role: secondary text: lede support, breadcrumb, sidebar section headers, on-this-page title, footer copy; holds 4.5:1 as real body-scale text, not just large text

hairline.role: decorative 1px borders: sidebar edge, section rules, table cell walls, code-block frame. Decorative only; carries no meaning alone, so it is exempt from the 3:1 UI floor

edge.role: interactive border: the search field, the sidebar toggle, the copy button, any stroke that must read as a control; holds the 3:1 UI-component floor on ground-0, ground-1 and ground-2

accent.role: THE one accent: prose links, sidebar active label, on-this-page active item, focus ring, heading-anchor affordance. A mid trust-blue (color.md); meets 4.5:1 on every ground and on both admonition tints

accent-hover.role: darker accent for link and control hover/active; higher contrast still, never lighter than the resting accent

cta.role: the filled Sign in button in the top nav; white text on the accent surface at 6.01:1. The only filled accent surface in the chrome; emphasis is contrast, not a second hue (patterns.md, isolation effect)

note.role: the note admonition: a calm teal, deliberately off the blue accent so a note never reads as a link. Ink holds 4.5:1 on the note tint; the 3px left edge holds 3:1 on the page ground as a meaningful graphic

warn.role: the warning admonition: an amber, distinct from both accent and note. Amber sits at hue ~40, well clear of the chartreuse/dark-yellow trough (color.md). Ink holds 4.5:1 on the amber tint; the left edge holds 3:1 on the ground

heading_sans.stack: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
heading_sans.role: the clear sans for the top nav, the page h1, and every h2/h3. Sentence case, quiet weight; headings anchor the document, they do not shout

body_sans.stack: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, Roboto, 'Noto Sans', 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
body_sans.role: the humanist sans for prose. Paragraphs cap at roughly 65ch so the eye can return to the next line without hunting; this measure is the core of the reading experience

mono_code.stack: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', 'Roboto Mono', monospace
mono_code.role: literal code, inline and block; never tracked, never uppercased. Inline code sits on a tinted chip; block code sits on the ground-2 surface with its own frame and a copy control

label.stack: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
label.role: microlabels: sidebar section headers, the ON THIS PAGE title, the admonition titles, breadcrumb separators. Tracked uppercase sans; it labels, it does not read as prose

layout_grammar.note: Ordered nav to footer in document reading order. Slot names are the section ids. The shell is a three-column grid at desktop (sidebar / prose / rail) that degrades to a single column at phone width, where the sidebar collapses behind a visible toggle whose default no-JS state is open.

slots.name: top-nav
slots.contract: A sticky top bar, always visible, no hamburger at any width. Left: brand mark plus wordmark. Right: at most four primary destinations (Product, Docs active, API, Blog) as sentence-case sans links with a visible active state, then exactly one filled accent CTA (Sign in). Five items maximum. At phone width the destinations stay visible by wrapping or condensing; they never collapse into a disclosure menu.

slots.name: left-sidebar
slots.contract: A persistent left column listing doc sections as uppercase-label group headers, each with its pages as sans links. Exactly one page carries the active state: accent label, accent-soft fill, and a 3px accent left bar. A search field sits at the top with a real interactive border. The sidebar collapses behind a single visible toggle at phone width; the default no-JS state SHOWS the sidebar, and any hiding CSS is gated behind a JS-added marker class.

slots.name: prose-column
slots.contract: The reading column, capped near 65ch. A breadcrumb, an h1, a one-paragraph lede in muted ink, then h2 and h3 headings that are ANCHORED: each has an id and reveals a copy-link affordance on hover and on keyboard focus. Body paragraphs, lists, an inline-code chip style, and fenced code blocks that EITHER soft-wrap or scroll inside their own overflow-x:auto container. At least one realistically long install command proves the code line does not clip or widen the page at 390px.

slots.name: admonition
slots.contract: A callout component with a note variant (teal) and a warning variant (amber). Each has a tracked uppercase title, an icon built from inline SVG, a 3px left edge in its own hue, and a soft tinted background. Both hues are held distinct from the accent so neither reads as a link; links inside a callout still take the accent color and stay above 4.5:1 on the tint.

slots.name: on-this-page-rail
slots.contract: A right rail titled ON THIS PAGE listing the h2/h3 headings of the current document as in-page anchor links, with the current section marked active. Sticky within the viewport on desktop; hidden below the desktop breakpoint, which is acceptable because the same headings are reachable from the prose and the sidebar.

slots.name: pager-and-footer
slots.contract: A previous/next pager at the foot of the prose column: two bordered cards, each a whole-card link with a muted direction label and a sans page title, one or both present. Then a page footer with a top hairline: a brand column with a one-line mission, up to three link columns under uppercase-label headers, and a bottom bar with copyright and legal links. Every anchor has an explicit color.

assertions.note: The docs genre rules restated as machine-checkable tests, plus the hard accessibility floors. Every assertion is phrased so a script or audit can pass or fail it.

rules.assert: the page commits to one look: every tokens.grounds value has relative luminance >= 0.85 and is not #FFFFFF; no dark-scheme token family is left undefined
body text color is not #000000 and contrast(ink, every ground including code-inline) >= 4.5
the prose paragraph column max-width computes to roughly 60ch to 72ch; body line-height >= 1.6
fenced code-block surface (ground-2) and inline-code chip (code-inline) are distinct color values from the prose ground-0; code glyphs on each surface hold contrast >= 4.5
every fenced code block EITHER sets white-space:pre-wrap with word-break OR sets overflow-x:auto on its own container; a realistically long command line neither clips nor widens the page at 390px
every h2 and h3 in the prose column has an id and a focusable copy-link affordance revealed on hover and on :focus-within/:focus-visible
exactly one sidebar page link carries the active treatment (accent label plus accent-soft fill plus accent left bar) and it corresponds to the current document
top-nav primary destinations <= 5; hamburger/disclosure menus in the TOP nav == 0 at every width including 390px (the left sidebar toggle is a docs-sidebar affordance, not the primary nav)
note and warning hues are each a different value from the accent; neither equals the accent, so a callout never reads as a link
document.scrollingElement.scrollWidth <= 390 at a true 390px viewport (measured inside an iframe host, not a clamped headless window); flex and grid children set min-width:0
with scripts stripped, all content is visible and anchors navigate; the sidebar and on-this-page content are SHOWN, not hidden, because hiding CSS is gated behind a JS-added marker class
contrast >= 4.5:1 for all text including inline code and code blocks; >= 3:1 for interactive borders and meaningful graphics including admonition edges
the accent link color holds contrast >= 4.5 on ground-0, ground-1, ground-2, code-inline, accent-soft, note-bg and warn-bg wherever a link can appear
no ground or accent or admonition token falls in the chartreuse/dark-yellow hue band (hue 60-90 with lightness < 60%); the warning amber sits below hue 50
external network requests == 0; @font-face count == 0; all font-family values are system stacks; all imagery is CSS or inline SVG
every interactive element paints a visible outline on :focus-visible with contrast >= 3:1 against its surroundings; :focus:not(:focus-visible) suppresses the ring on pointer clicks
count of <a> elements without an explicit color rule in scope == 0; button-styled anchors set text-decoration:none
no container-scoped descendant selector (for example .sidebar a) outranks a component rule that shares that container; components own their color via their own class
every var(--x) referenced in the stylesheet has a definition in scope; no interactive element or graphic renders transparent for a missing token

the page-transition loader appears only after >= 300ms of pending navigation; it is a single centered indeterminate ring on the page ground; it starts hidden via the [hidden] attribute; under prefers-reduced-motion it is a static mark; it is visibly populated in every animation phase
under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, running animations == 0 and all content renders
count of U+2014 characters in every shipped file == 0
